title: Qatar vs. Switzerland World Cup Match Preview, Picks & Odds
original_text:
Qatar vs. Switzerland World Cup Match Preview, Picks & Odds
Saturday features four World Cup matches with Qatar taking on Switzerland to kick it all off. Can either club win the match and take a Group B lead following Canada vs. Bosnia & Herzegovina’s draw? Let’s go through the betting data, odds, trends and insights for Saturday’s first match from Levi’s Stadium.
Qatar vs. Switzerland World Cup Match Betting Analysis
Switzerland enters as the rightful favorite, winning three of four matches when coming off a draw while also seeing a draw in three of five matches. Qatar enters losing four of its last six matches. Switzerland to win, even cover -1.5-goals is expensive, so moving markets we’ll go to the BTS next. The trends go in opposite directs, with Switzerland hitting the BT in five of six matches while Qatar has seen the BTS fail in four of five matches.
In fact, Qatar has failed to score a goal in four of five matches, while also going Under the goal total in four of its last five matches. Although Switzerland has seen the BTS hit in recent matches, it has actually failed in its last four World Cup matches during the daytime. Switzerland has also gone Under the total in three of four World Cup matches during the daytime.
Qatar vs. Switzerland World Cup Match Best Bet, Pick & Prediction
Best Bet: QAT/SUI Under 2.5 (+115) at DraftKings
Score Prediction: Switzerland 2, Qatar 0
Qatar vs. Switzerland World Cup Match Information
Date: Saturday, June 13, 2026
Time: 3:00 p.m. ET
Network: FOX
Location: Santa Clara, California
Venue: Levi’s Stadium
Group: Group B

Not in a Legal Sports Betting State? Consider Prediction Markets
If sports betting isn’t available in your state, prediction markets offer another way to participate in sports-related events. Unlike traditional sportsbooks, prediction markets allow users to buy and sell contracts based on the outcome of real-world events, with prices fluctuating based on market sentiment. Bettors have a chance to take early exits on their positions, providing an opportunity to secure profit before an event is settled.
Several platforms have expanded their sports offerings in recent years, including Kalshi, Polymarket, and Fanatics Markets. These sites allow users to trade contracts on outcomes ranging from championship winners and award races to individual game results, depending on the platform and available markets.
Prediction markets operate under different regulatory frameworks than traditional sportsbooks, and availability can vary by state and platform. Before signing up, be sure to check each site’s eligibility requirements and terms of service to confirm whether you can participate where you live.
